title: Dean Richard's Annual Report
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## January 31, 2021 — Dean Richard Lawson Annual Report

LITURGICAL CONTEXT
January 31, 2021 falls in the Fourth Sunday after the Epiphany in the church calendar. The RCL readings for this Sunday would have been 1 Corinthians 13:1-13 (the love chapter), Psalm 112, Luke 4:21-30, and Jeremiah 1:4-10. The congregation was in Ordinary Time following the Christmas/Epiphany season, a liturgical period emphasizing discipleship and ministry.

EPISCOPAL/ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS
The Episcopal Church was in the period following the contentious 79th General Convention (held virtually in July 2020), where decisions on LGBTQ+ inclusion and church structure had created significant division within dioceses nationwide. The Diocese of Colorado was navigating the aftermath of these decisions while managing pandemic-related operational challenges. No major episcopal elections in the Rocky Mountain region were imminent at this specific date.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS
Denver was experiencing an unusually warm and dry winter, with temperatures in the 50s-60s Fahrenheit during late January. The city remained under COVID-19 restrictions with indoor dining and worship capacity limits in effect. The Colorado state legislature was in its early session days (convenes in early January), with pandemic recovery and vaccine distribution dominating state-level discussions. The Capitol Hill neighborhood, where the cathedral is located, remained economically stressed from the pandemic's impact on small businesses.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)
President Biden had been in office for ten days, having been inaugurated on January 20, 2021. The second impeachment of President Trump was proceeding in the Senate, with the trial scheduled to begin the following week. The US death toll from COVID-19 had passed 425,000, with vaccine rollout accelerating but facing significant distribution challenges. The stock market was at record highs despite economic uncertainty, and the GameStop/meme stock phenomenon was at its peak, dominating financial news.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS
The COVID-19 pandemic was devastating Europe, with the UK, Germany, and France under strict lockdowns and facing questions about vaccine supply. Brazil was emerging as a new epicenter of the pandemic with the Manaus variant causing concern. Myanmar's military had conducted a coup d'état on February 1 (the day after this sermon), so tensions were rising in that region heading into this week.

CULTURAL MOMENT
The national mood was one of cautious hope mixed with pandemic fatigue and deep political division. The January 6 Capitol riot aftermath was still dominating consciousness, with debates about accountability and healing sharply polarized. Many Americans were isolated and grieving, with over 400,000 COVID deaths creating a sense of collective loss. The Super Bowl LV was scheduled for February 7, and in the Denver area, the Broncos' disappointing 2020 season had just concluded, shifting local sports attention to the Nuggets and Avalanche.
